description:
# Why

Status is derivable — **bl-4778 RESOLVED it out of core by subtraction**: "there is **no `state`/`status`
field at all**. Status is a DERIVED VIEW computed by a short-circuit ladder over exactly THREE live
states: `claimant` set ⇒ claimed (claim-blockers not even evaluated); else unresolved claim-blocker ⇒
blocked; else ⇒ ready." A stored status "would have zero core behavior (`ready()` never reads it),
making it a single-valued tag — so it folds away." Core is right and stays UNCHANGED.

But the absence drives every external integrator insane: a dashboard, an issue mirror, or any tool
reading raw `tasks/<id>.md` or `bl ... --json` finds no status — it must reimplement the ladder (and
the close-blocker nuance) itself. bl-4778 already named the escape hatch: "A team wanting a stored,
ordered pipeline opts in OUTSIDE core via an unknown preserved `state:` key + a display plugin (§3) —
severable, never a core field." §3 repeats it verbatim.

This ball SPECS that display plugin as a shipped DEFAULT capability — alongside `tracker` and
`delivery`, "default capabilities + reference implementations, invoked identically to any third party"
(§6). **Spec only; build is a follow-up.**

# What it is

`status` — a default, off-core plugin that materializes core's derived ladder into a stored `status`
string field on each task, ENRICHED with the one distinction core deliberately folded: the gate/PR case.

It is a DELIBERATE, SCOPED DENORMALIZATION — a cache, not a source. Core's derived ladder (§3) remains
the single source of truth; `bl` itself NEVER reads `status` (the one hardcoded guard reads `claimant`,
§3). The plugin is the SOLE writer and owns freshness end-to-end. Severable: delete its symlinks + the
field and core is untouched (severability test). The line holds only as long as `bl` never reads it.

# Vocabulary (the field's values)

The enrichment over core's 3-ladder is splitting "claimed" by close-blocker presence — the very thing
§3 folded ("a claimed ball with an open gate is just claimed ... 'in review' was never functional").
Integrators want it back; the PLUGIN, not core, carries that opinion:

- `open` — unclaimed, no unresolved claim-blocker (= core "ready")
- `blocked` — unclaimed, ≥1 unresolved claim-blocker
- `in_progress` — claimed, no open close-blocker
- `in_review` — claimed, ≥1 open close-blocker (the PR/gate case, §10)

(closed/dropped are absence, not a value — file deleted + `bl-op`, consistent with core §9. The field
never holds a terminal value.)

# How it reaches consumers — RESOLVED: persist + free passthrough (NO read hook)

NOT by intercepting reads. Per §7 "There is no return channel: ... stdout is diagnostics only," and §16
"nothing is parsed back into state" — a read hook CANNOT inject a column into balls' own `bl list`/
`bl show` table; it can only append its own output. So there is **no read hook**.

Instead the field is PERSISTED, and §3's lossless export does the rest: frontmatter "exports
deterministically and losslessly to JSON for external tooling" and "Unknown keys preserved on
writeback." So `bl show --json` / `bl list --json` carry `status` FOR FREE, and raw-file readers see it
directly. (Core's human `bl list` already renders the live 3-ladder per §3; the plugin adds the
persisted, 4-value field for everyone NOT going through `bl`.)

# When it's written (the writer)

Single writer, PRE-SEAL, riding the op's one commit (so it never violates §8's "post reactors ... DO
NOT edit the ball — it is sealed"). Wire `NN-status` into the `pre` phase of the mutating ops that
change the ladder inputs: `create`, `claim`, `unclaim`, `update`. Each pre hook reads the §7 pre
payload (`current_state` + intended `command.field_changes`), computes the RESULTING status from a
single shared derive fn, and writes `status = "..."` into the staged `tasks/*.md` frontmatter before
seal. No extra commit, no post-mutation. `close`/`drop` delete the file — nothing to write on the
closing task itself.

# Cross-task freshness — the drift hole — RESOLVED: fan-out

THE design hole, stated honestly: a task's derived status can change with NO op on it. Blocker edges are
the only cross-task coupling; the only uncaught transition is a claim-blocker RESOLVING — when task X
closes/drops, every task with `blocker{id:X, on:claim}` may flip `blocked → open`, but no op touches
them, so their stored field drifts. A WRONG cache is worse than none (it is the exact integrator pain we
are fixing), so we pay for correctness:

- `close.post` and `drop.post` enumerate `{ t : t has blocker{id:<closing-id>, on:claim} }` and shell
  back `bl update <t>` for each — re-sealing that task so its own `status/pre` re-derives. (Adding or
  removing a blocker is the task's OWN `update`, already caught by pre.)
- Bounded: fan-out is exactly the blocker edge set, ONE hop (a dependent's status change resolves no
  one else's blocker, so no cascade). Recursion capped by `BALLS_PLUGIN_DEPTH` — §6 "At the cap, nested
  ops run PLUGIN-FREE."

# Attack — what this still doesn't solve

- **New field smell (§0).** A stored `status` is exactly the "new field is a smell." Defense: zero core
  behavior, lives entirely off-core, the §3-sanctioned opt-in. Not a core field.
- **Backfill.** Tasks created before install have no `status` until their next op. Reads stay correct
  (core derives); raw-file readers see absence, not wrongness. Option: a `doctor.post` check in the
  plugin's own §16 territory that reports drift/absence and names `bl update <id>` to refresh — no new
  verb (§16 delegation pattern). Decide in build: ship the doctor check and/or a one-shot backfill.
- **Two computations of one ladder** (core's render + the plugin's field). Acceptable: if they diverge,
  core wins by definition and the field is just stale-or-wrong cache, surfaced by the doctor check.
- **Field name.** `status` vs `state` — §3/bl-4778 use `state:` as the example key. Pick one in build;
  `status` matches the user's vocabulary and the rendered column. Any unknown key rides through `update`.

# Scope

Spec only. Build (a reference binary + seed wiring in the `default-config/` folder, §4) is a follow-up
under the greenfield impl epic (bl-72a8), naturally gated on the read verbs (bl-20fc) and the
`default-config/` seed (bl-4e14) existing.
title: Topic: default plugin 'status' — persist the derived ladder as a stored field for integrators
